text: [COMPETE] CSS selector parser alongside grap: support >> [role=button][name=Save] syntax for external users, grap remains internal power syntax
[COMPETE] Scenario step expect + recovery: Action-Expect-Recovery pattern per step, auto-retry with recovery handler before failing
[COMPETE] Experience DB sharing: export/import mechanism for community app profiles (like DirectShell vision of shared configs)
[COMPETE] Rust performance evaluation: benchmark UIA enumeration .NET vs Rust, consider native module for hot paths

[BUG] ValuePattern.Value read-back empty/stale after SetValue on Windows 11 Notepad RichEditD2DPT — expect value_contains fails immediately after successful set_value. ReadElementValue returns old content. Likely RichEditD2DPT caches Value or doesn't implement GetValue properly. Workaround: use TextPattern for doc content instead of ValuePattern.

[FEATURE] a11y close shared close-all helper: 'wkappbot a11y close {proc:X} --all --show-dialogs' — first runs find to list target windows + detect modals, prompts user-style output before actual close. Avoids the user-Notepad session-restore trap.

rootCause: Duplicate entries across DG-WKAppBot and DGWCS-WKAppBot.CLI origins — same recovery task. Copy suggestions.jsonl + experience/ from backup to current hq.

rootCause: Duplicate across origins — audit committed files for secrets/PII, strip hq data from tracking, scrub commit history, finalize LICENSE/README/AGENTS before public release.

title: [BUG] Korean argv CP949 corruption from Bash (3 fix options: CommandLineToArgvW / --from-file / --stdin)
text: [BUG] Korean argv CP949 corruption from Bash (3 fix options: CommandLineToArgvW / --from-file / --stdin)
rootCause: Git Bash UTF-8 argv reinterpreted as CP949 by CreateProcessA on Korean Windows

[BUG] win-click coordinate system ambiguous. Docs say x y relative to window-title-matched window, but observed behavior: passing absolute screen coords (107, 1373) and window-relative coords (15, 1089) BOTH return rc=0 but neither hits intended button. Workaround: undocumented; trial and error. Fix: (a) add --abs flag for explicit absolute screen coords, (b) document client vs window vs screen coord choice, (c) log resolved click coords (post-transform) for debugging.

title: [BUG] screenshot action ignores params.path + target.class_name
text: [BUG] screenshot action ignores params.path + target.class_name
rootCause: Playbook screenshot action neither honors params.path for output file nor target.class_name for window selection
estimatedWork: Wire params.path into screenshot output path; resolve target.class_name through WindowFinder before capture

title: [FEATURE] Add read + shell actions to playbook engine
text: [FEATURE] Add read + shell actions to playbook engine
rootCause: Playbook has 20 actions but no read (UIA/OCR text extraction) or shell (external command run); blocks end-to-end scenarios that need verification reads or pre/post-steps
estimatedWork: Implement ReadAction (delegates to A11yCommand.Read) + ShellAction (AppBotPipe.StartTracked wrapper); add schema entries

[SKILL-REVIEW for codex] skill codex-cli-wkappbot-usage v1.2 needs operational depth upgrade. Reviewer: Claude WkAutoQuant agent. Current rating 3/5 — orientation OK, production reference incomplete. Specific gaps + recommendations:

STRENGTHS to keep:
(1) ChatGPT account sign-in vs API key distinction is accurate and essential for new users.
(2) Separate-conversation-mental-model caveat (codex session is not the claude chat transcript) prevents real confusion.
(3) Repo-local bin + symlink preference over global install is good hygiene.
(4) Limits are plan-specific, not account-identity based — important correction.
(5) Tool triage guidance (ask / skill / eye / codex) is useful decision-tree.

WEAKNESSES to fix:
(1) No concrete flag examples. Skill mentions only codex --help, --version, --login. Missing: print mode equivalent, prompt injection flags, output format flags, session resume flags, max turns / budget caps, cache controls. Ref: Claude CLI skill claude-cli-automation-patterns-wkappbot-integration v1.0 has 14 operational patterns with exact flag trios.

(2) No rc / error handling patterns. Production automation needs: exit code semantics, silent-failure signatures, retry policies, timeout handling. Claude skill documents rc=1-but-success trap (artifact verification pattern) + argv length limit + Korean CP949 encoding bypass. Codex equivalent patterns are missing — document them after real usage data accumulates.

(3) Steps are paragraphs not actionable rules. Rewrite with 'Do: [exact command]. Expect: [concrete output/behavior]. If: [failure mode] → [remediation].' pattern. Abstract 'explain limits carefully' step (6) needs to become 'codex --limits / codex status / <actual command> returns current plan quota'.

(4) Integration examples absent. Mention 'wkappbot ask / skill / eye / codex' but no actual chained workflow code. Add 1-2 examples: e.g., 'wkappbot ask codex <q>' or 'codex invokes wkappbot a11y during a task'.

(5) Author = codex (self-description). Skill lacks outside-perspective battle scars. Expand via: collect 3-5 real failure cases from user sessions + document workarounds.

(6) No streaming / session resumption guidance. If Codex supports --resume / --stream-json equivalents, document them for long-running tasks.

(7) Cost monitoring missing. ChatGPT Plus (20/mo) vs Pro (200/mo) usage differences, Codex-specific quota tracking, overuse behavior.

Target: skill v2.0 = 10-15 steps, each with exact invocation + expected result + known failure + fix. Match Claude CLI skill depth.

Priority: medium (skill exists, just thin). Not blocking but improves next user's ramp-up significantly.

Delegate source file splitting to Codex CLI (token optimization)

WHY: Claude Opus sessions burn tokens re-reading 1500-3000 line files.
24+ .cs files exceed the 400-line CLAUDE.md recommendation. Delegating
mechanical split to Codex (ChatGPT Plus plan) or other low-cost model
saves Opus tokens for design decisions and complex refactors.

TIER 1 TARGETS (established split patterns + clean boundaries):
- A11yCommand.cs (1643) -> split per sub-action; A11yCommand.Cdp.cs exists as pattern
- SkillCommand.cs (1272) -> split per subcommand (list/read/search/contribute/edit/audit/verify/install/export/import/delete)
- ChatCommand.cs (1213) -> .Resolvers.cs and .ShellDefaults.cs already done; extract shell dispatch, REPL, chat-loop
- AppBotEyeGlobalMode.cs (1744) -> .FileWatchers.cs and .Tick.cs already done; extract hot-swap + pipe server + signal handlers

MANDATORY CONSTRAINTS (learned from 2026-04-20 rogue edit incident):
1. git add each new file in the SAME turn. 2026-04-20 found 5 files on disk referenced by HEAD for days but never tracked (FocusSafe / ApprovalPolicy / ChatCommand.Resolvers / ChatCommand.ShellDefaults / AppBotEyeGlobalMode.Types). Fresh clone would have failed to build. Rule now standing.
2. Logic-preserving MOVE only. No behavioral changes bundled. Reject substitutions like AppBotPipe.StartTracked to Process.Start disguised as refactor.
3. Build must pass after each file split. Verify with dotnet publish before commit.
4. One file per commit (or at most 2 tightly related) for review granularity.
5. Preserve comments verbatim. The WHY in original comments is institutional memory.

STANDARD COMMAND TEMPLATE:
  wkappbot ask codex "Split <path> into partials grouped by <boundary>. Constraints: logic-preserving move only, git add each new file, build must pass, one file per commit. Preserve all comments verbatim. List the plan before executing."

OR multi-turn via exec stdin-inject:
  wkappbot exec codex --stdin-inject   (then feed step-by-step instructions)

ANTI-PATTERNS TO REJECT (flag in PR review):
- Silent logic changes bundled with move
- New files missing from git add
- Extracted partial not compiling
- Over-aggressive split that fragments one logical concern across 5+ files

START ORDER: A11yCommand.cs first (biggest Tier-1 + cleanest sub-action boundary + existing partial pattern).

MODEL CHOICE: Low-cost OK (Codex ChatGPT Plus, Haiku, etc). Build verification is the safety net; model size does not change correctness requirement.

BUILD VERIFY (REQUIRED READING FOR DELEGATE):
  skill id = wkappbot-build-verify-workflow (v1.0)
  wkappbot skill read wkappbot-build-verify-workflow
One-liner: C:/Program Files/dotnet/dotnet publish D:/GitHub/WKAppBot/csharp/src/WKAppBot.CLI/WKAppBot.CLI.csproj -c Release --verbosity minimal
Green signature: last 3 lines include "wkappbot-core.new.exe staged for hot-swap" + "skills installed to HQ", exit 0.
Delegate must run this after each file split AND before git commit.

TOKEN COST NOTE: source splitting itself burns tokens (reading 1500-3000 line files for move-only ops). Mitigations: (1) process one Tier-1 file per session, (2) Codex / Haiku is cheaper than Opus for the read-heavy move, (3) specify explicit target line ranges so the agent does not re-scan whole file repeatedly.
